description_long: This is a small, single-story cabin with a simple rectangular footprint and a dark brown gable roof. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely finished in wood or painted siding, and the structure sits alone on a flat, circular base. The front facade features a centered door flanked by two small, symmetrically placed windows, giving the cabin a balanced and inviting appearance. The roof is steeply pitched, typical of gable designs, and overhangs slightly on all sides. No porch or veranda is present, and the windows and door are the only visible architectural details. The overall style is modest and functional, reminiscent of a rustic or traditional countryside cabin.
description: A small, light-colored rectangular cabin with a dark brown gable roof, centered door, and two front windows. The design is simple, balanced, and rustic.
description_view_0: The front of the cabin is visible, showing a centered door and two symmetrically placed windows beneath a dark gable roof.
description_view_1: The side of the cabin is shown, revealing a plain wall with no windows or doors and the full length of the gable roof.
description_view_2: The rear of the cabin is visible, featuring a blank wall with no openings and the back slope of the gable roof.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the cabin is shown, mirroring the other side with a plain wall and the gable roof extending above.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a steep pitch and slight overhangs, covered in dark material
attribute_window_arrangement: Two small, rectangular windows symmetrically placed on the front facade, one on each side of the door; no windows visible on other sides
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade between the two windows
